$description: Input component tokens — text input, textarea, select. Third layer: maps semantic color tokens to specific input roles and states. Covers default, hover, focus, error, and disabled states for both value and placeholder text.

input.$description: Form input fields — text input, textarea, select. System leans on border transitions and focus ring for state communication; background changes are subtle.

default.$description: Input rests on the page canvas — same color as the page background. Creates depth via border + label contrast, not background elevation.

hover.$description: Background unchanged on hover — border change alone communicates interactivity.

focus.$description: Background unchanged on focus — the focus ring carries the state signal.

disabled.$description: Disabled input sits on the elevated surface color — visually recedes, communicates non-interactivity.

error.$description: Error state background unchanged — border color alone communicates the error.

value.$description: User-entered text — primary foreground, full legibility.

placeholder.$description: Placeholder text — tertiary foreground. Minimum legible level. Not disabled — disabled has its own token.

label.$description: Form field label above the input — secondary foreground, clearly associated but not primary content.

label-required.$description: Required field asterisk or indicator — danger color signals mandatory without alarming.

helper.$description: Helper text below the input — same as placeholder, tertiary level.

error.$description: Inline error message text below the input. Red signals the validation failure.

disabled.$description: Text in disabled input — deeply receded, signals non-interactivity.

default.$description: Resting input border — the same as all UI edges.

hover.$description: Input border on hover — steps up one level to signal interactivity.

focus.$description: Input border on focus — phosphor green focus ring, maximum visibility for keyboard navigation.

error.$description: Input border in error state — red communicates validation failure clearly.

disabled.$description: Input border when disabled — recedes to near-canvas.

radius.$description: Input corner radius — subtle 4px rounding. Softer than a pure rectangle but maintains the structured, precise character of the system.

x.$description: Horizontal padding inside the input — 16px left/right. Provides comfortable typing clearance from the border.

y.$description: Vertical padding inside the input — 8px top/bottom. Combined with line-height creates the standard 40px input height.
